proved: Restore requires the exact live editor revision, exact current immutable head, valid target lineage, project identity, and non-empty researcher attribution before mutation.
Restore replaces exact semantic blocks without reparsing paragraph or policy text as markup.
The restored snapshot is committed as a new immutable child of the current head; historical versions are never rewritten.
The editor-root mutation, new immutable version record, and head pointer are performed in one Yjs transaction.
A deliberately partial synthetic editor failure restores the prior draft and shared head before the transaction completes.
Stale document input invokes no editor mutation and creates no version.
Rollback preserves a canonical version record that appears during asynchronous commit preparation instead of deleting a collaborator's identical record.
A real two-peer pinned Lexical/Yjs Memory-provider fixture receives root, version record, and head in one shared update, then converges its rendered editor projection.
The product rail exposes a two-step Prepare restore / Restore as new version confirmation, a cancel action, and copy stating that existing versions remain unchanged.

The complete workspace suite, complete frontend suite, production frontend build, 41-check structural audit, and packaged Tauri build pass under the bounded operation watchdog; the log confirms Compiling app v0.1.10.
notProved: Drive or WebSocket restore transport and two-install behavior.
Crash or power-loss injection at the IndexedDB durability boundary.
Instantaneous rendered-UI atomicity; the shared Yjs state is atomic and the peer Lexical projection catches up after that transaction.
Automatic reconciliation when another collaborator advances the document or history head; restore fails closed and requires retry.
Packaged pointer, focus, keyboard, screen-reader, narrow-layout, or dark-theme interaction.
MCP-triggered restore, authenticated participant identity, archive interchange, or a signed updater release.
